Description: Apply L274 to any Meals and Entertainment expense account because IRC §274 limits or disallows the tax deduction (typically 50% for meals and 0% for entertainment); Meals Expense; Entertainment Expense; Client Hospitality

Apply L162m to any executive compensation account because IRC §162(m) limits the deductible amount (generally $1 million per covered employee); Executive Salaries; Executive Bonuses; Stock-based compensation for top executives
Apply L163j to any interest expense account because IRC §163(j) may limit how much interest can be deducted, with excess carried forward; Interest Expense; Loan Interest; Bond Interest
Apply L243 to any dividend income from corporate equity investments because IRC §243 allows a partial deduction (typically 50%–65%); Dividend Income; Equity Investment Income

Apply L274 (general) to accounts where book and tax recognize the same item in different periods, creating a temporary timing difference; Depreciation Expense; Accrued Expenses (warranty, bonus, etc.); Deferred Revenue
Notes: Some systems use L274 broadly for general timing differences beyond Meals & Entertainment.

Description: Apply T+ when taxable income is higher than book income in the current period, meaning the company will get a tax benefit later; Accrued liabilities (not yet deductible for tax); Warranty expense; Pension expense
Notes: Book recognizes expense earlier than tax.

Description: Apply T- when taxable income is lower than book income in the current period, meaning the company will pay more tax later; Depreciation (accelerated for tax); Amortization differences
Notes: Tax recognizes expense earlier than book.

Description: Apply P+ to any expense recorded for book purposes that is not deductible for tax, permanently increasing taxable income; Fines and penalties; Certain meals/entertainment; Lobbying expenses

Apply P- to any income recorded for book purposes that is not taxable, permanently decreasing taxable income; Municipal bond interest; Certain tax-exempt income; Some dividend income (if not using L243)
